post_text: Kubernetes autoscaling traps caught us off guard. We thought we had it under control, but our cluster was overwhelmed. Our production scenario involved a high-traffic e-commerce platform, with sudden spikes in demand. The problem was that our autoscaling configuration was not tuned for this kind of workload, leading to a significant delay in scaling up. This was due to the technical root cause of not having the correct metrics in place to trigger the autoscaling. The engineering solution involved implementing a custom metrics adapter using Prometheus and Grafana to monitor our workload, and adjusting our autoscaling configuration to account for these new metrics. We also had to make some tough trade-offs between scaling speed and resource utilization. The lessons learned here are to carefully consider your autoscaling strategy and make sure you have the right metrics in place. Now, what are some common pitfalls you've encountered with Kubernetes autoscaling, and how did you overcome them? #Kubernetes #DevSecOps #CloudSecurity #PlatformEngineering
